mission.title: Install and Configure Cartography on Kubernetes
mission.description: Cartography is a Python tool that consolidates infrastructure assets and their relationships in an intuitive graph view powered by a Neo4j database. Installing Cartography allows you to explore and automate security scenarios, helping to identify risks and improve security posture.

steps.title: Add the Cartography Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Cartography Helm repository to your Helm configuration:
```bash
helm repo add cartography https://cartography-cncf.github.io/cartography/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Cartography using Helm
steps.description:
Install Cartography in a new namespace called 'cartography':
```bash
helm install cartography cartography/cartography --namespace cartography --create-namespace --version 0.130.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Cartography pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace cartography
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Update the Cartography deployment to set resource limits:
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment cartography --namespace cartography --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

steps.title: Access the Cartography web interface
steps.description:
Port-forward the Cartography service to access the web interface:
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/cartography --namespace cartography 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the Cartography pods running and accessible via the web interface at http://localhost:8080. You should also see the resource limits applied to the deployment.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add cartography https://cartography-cncf.github.io/cartography/charts
helm repo update
kubectl set resources deployment cartography --namespace cartography --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Cartography Helm release from the cluster using the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall cartography --namespace cartography
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) associated with Cartography:
```bash
kubectl delete crd cartography --namespace cartography
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace cartography
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the Cartography namespace and verify that it has been removed:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace cartography
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of Cartography by exporting the existing configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace cartography -o yaml > cartography-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade Cartography to the latest version:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade cartography cartography/cartography --namespace cartography --version 0.140.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the Cartography pods are running with the new version:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace cartography
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Cartography pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace cartography
```
Investigate the logs for any configuration issues or missing dependencies.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Cartography web interface, ensure the service is running:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace cartography
```
If the service is not running, check the deployment status:
```bash
kubectl get deployments --namespace cartography
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter issues related to insufficient resources, check the resource usage:
```bash
kubectl top pods --namespace cartography
```
Consider adjusting the resource limits or requests in the deployment.

troubleshooting.title: Helm release not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you receive an error stating the Helm release is not found, verify the release name and namespace:
```bash
helm list --namespace cartography
```
Ensure that the release name 'cartography' exists in the specified namespace.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your local machine.
